title: The Macro Minute: Will legal checks and balances fuel more Paradigm C upside?

Happy Tuesday out there, team 42. It's
your skipper here at Darius Dell to
present our macro minute for Tuesday,
September 2nd, 2025. Welcome back from
the holiday. Hope everyone had a
wonderful vacation. So, as always, we'll
start with the executive summary from
today's leadoff warning note. If you
would like to review the analysis
supporting these conclusions and to see
if you need to make any changes in your
portfolio to remain on the right side of
market risk or to get on the right side
of market risk to the extent you haven't
been following Kiss or Dr. Mo, you need
to be a client of 42 macro. So today's
key macro question is will legal checks
and balances fuel more paradigm C
upside? Our answer the Trump
administration has taken a few losses in
court recently. For example, a federal
appeals court recently ruled most of
Trump's reciprocal tariffs illegal under
the AIA statute. Moreover, a US district
court ruled Trump's deployment of
National Guard troops in Los Angeles
during immigration protests violated the
Posi Kamatitis Act. The US District
Court also blocked Trump's expanded
expedited removal policy, which allowed
nationwide deportations for undocumented
immigrants in the US for less than two
years. The developing upside risk to our
already 100%ile bullish paradigm C bull
case would be an effective display of
checks and balances that limit the worst
of the Trump economic agenda from
negatively impacting the US and global
economies via adverse trade and
immigration policies. While this may be
wishful thinking given the Supreme
Court's 6-3 Conservative majority and
three Trump appointees, Trump's
dissatisfaction with his appointed Fed
Chair J. Pal offers investors a glimmer
of hope. Sure, there are other legal
authorities the administration can use
to pursue Trump's trade policy agenda
that are more durable but take longer to
implement, such as the sections um 232
and 301 tariffs. Any such delayed
implementation would post push the bulk
of the stagflationary shock from tariffs
from the second half of 2025 into the
first half of 2026 and give the Fed more
scuts to cope or more scope to cut
interest rates in the interim. 1 H 2026
also coincides with the start of the
multi-year run of peak positive fiscal
impulse from the one big ugly bill. This
would mean the economy slows less
sharply in the second half of 2025 and
recovers less sharply throughout 2026.
It would also mean the big correction
everyone is waiting for this fall may
fail to materialize materialize ahead of
a likely Santa Claus rally. The likely
year-end performance chase would be
fueled by the upside capitulation of
investors who have yet to adequately
position for our paradigm CV. Elsewhere,
Treasury Secretary Scott Besson floated
the idea of declaring a national housing
emergency. This would provide the
administration some political cover to
continue overstepping legal boundaries
and meddling with the Fed. Refer to our
August 26 leadoff morning note for a
detailed overview of the steps the
administration are likely to take to
resuscitate the belleaguered housing
market. Each step is resoundingly
bullish for as risk asset prices.
Transitioning to our 42 micro dashboard.

from our community. This one's titled
market timing. Says, team42 macro. I'm a
new subscriber and a retiree 15 years
into retirement. The US's large and
growing federal debt and the declining
purchasing power of the US dollar with
my preliminary primary attractions to
KISS. I'm committed to not lose as much
purchasing power in the next decade as
we did in the last. That said, I'm
struggling to get comfortable with the
concept of buy and sell signals. All my
experience and education tells me the
market timing can't be successful in the
long run. I think the old saying goes

lost time in the markets than has than
have lost been lost in all the
corrections. Uh any words of advice? Are
there any 42 macro team members who just
run with the 603010 and rebalancing?

timing signals have helped me avoid the

investing activity. So, uh, couldn't be

are saying about us. So, definitely take
some time, uh, to review our
testimonials page if you, like the, um,
like the the person who asked the
question earlier have any, uh, uh, you
know, reservations, if you will, about
participating in our KISS system. And if
you have different reservations about
participating in our KISS system, just
remember KISS, you can customize KISS to
suit your liking. You don't have to have
60% stocks, 30% gold, and 10% Bitcoin.
You can swap out Bitcoin with something
that's lower beta. You can swap out gold
with something that has a higher yield
like fixed income securities and just
manage uh your customized version of
KISS that is more suitable for your risk
tolerance and and and and strategic
investment objectives um you know using
our uh the signals in our Dr. Mo uh uh
uh uh model. So Dr. Mo has um you know
sort of for lack of a better word buy
hold signals for uh 70 different
factors. So you know you can pick those
70 different factors and allocate the
across them accordingly uh in the
context of our uh KISS portfolio
construction framework uh you know to
the extent you don't want to follow uh
the standard KISS portfolio or if you
deem it to be uh unsuitable for your
specific investment needs. Uh so just be
aware of that. You know, you can
implement a customized version of KISS
or you can just trust that that the
standard version of KISS is doing
exactly what it's supposed to be doing,
which is helping investors all around
the world maximize upside capture in
bull markets and minimize downside
capture in bare markets in a repeatable
manner that allows them to remain on the
right side of market risk for a long
enough period of time so that they can
retire on time and comfortably. not just
comfortably in terms of how much money
they have to spend in their retirement
and leave their family behind when they
go to the great beyond, but also
comfortably in terms of the journey that
they're experiencing along the way in
terms of avoiding uh significant draw
downs in their portfolio. You know, the
types of draw downs that cause you to uh
stay up at night and and and um and and
create, you know, obviously some
consternation in your in your in your
family life and and work life. So, uh
we'll wrap it up there. I think people
get it. Uh if you don't get it, um spend
some time on that testimonials page. And
if uh you don't get it after that,
you're probably never going to get it.
So wrap it up there. Have a great day.
We'll catch you back here tomorrow.
